name: The Seven-Day Shared Game
audience: People with a meaningful 30-90 day outcome who are willing to let evidence contradict their self-story.

emails.subject: Your goal is not the game yet
emails.body: You do not need a better vision. You need one outcome, one scoreboard, and seven days of evidence. Reply with the result you want and the date by which movement should be visible.

emails.subject: Name the outcome
emails.body: Choose one outcome that matters within 30 days. Not a category. Not a mood. A result someone else could recognize. Complete: By [date], I will [outcome].

emails.subject: What would count as evidence?
emails.body: Ask: what could a skeptical observer see, hear, or inspect if this were moving? Write one metric, artifact, event, or behavior. If nothing could count, you have a wish, not a game.

emails.subject: Let the mirror be inconvenient
emails.body: Give the guide your goal and the last seven days of relevant actions. Ask what your behavior implies you actually prioritize. Do not explain the contradiction before recording it.

emails.subject: Choose a seven-day probe
emails.body: Pick the smallest repeated action that could generate new evidence. Include a trigger, a frequency, and a failure condition. You are designing a probe, not a new personality.

emails.subject: Add a witness
emails.body: Choose a person, public receipt, or scheduled check that can make the action visible. Private intention is easy to revise. A witness makes the record heavier.

emails.subject: Do not improve the result
emails.body: Run the experiment and record what happened. Include avoidance, friction, surprises, and misses. The result is not a performance review. It is the instrument reading.

emails.subject: Update, do not celebrate too early
emails.body: What did reality falsify? Choose continue, modify, or stop. State the next experiment and the evidence that would change your mind. Completion means an update, not a victory lap.

emails.subject: Should we run the next game together?
emails.body: If the game produced a real contradiction and you want help designing the next probe, reply with your outcome, evidence, and repeating obstacle. That is enough to see whether coaching is useful.
